items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now I have a complete picture of the feature. Let me check the Playwright config and then write the test file.

Now I have everything I need. Let me create the E2E test file:  **Data-testid contract:** - `quick-view-btn` — overlay bar button on product tiles - `quick-view-modal` — modal content container   - `quick-view-spinner` — loading spinner in modal - `quick-view-error` — error state in modal
Now let me run the self-review gate to check for banned patterns:
No banned patterns found. Let me also verify the file was written correctly:
Good. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The `e2e-author` phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## E2E Test File: `e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts`  ### 14 Tests Across 5 Test Suites  | Suite | Tests | Coverage | |---|---|---| | **Overlay Bar on PLP** (3) | Quick View buttons exist on tiles, accessible `aria-label`, semantic `<button>` element | Verifies the `quick-view-btn` testid contract | | **Modal Interaction** (5) | Opens on click, loading spinner resolves, correct `data-testid` + `aria-label`, product details display (heading, price, Add to Cart), "View Full Details" link | Verifies `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error` testid contracts | | **Modal Close** (2) | Close via X button, close via Escape key | Both Chakra modal close mechanisms | | **Navigation** (2) | Quick View doesn't navigate away from PLP (URL unchanged), "View Full Details" link navigates to PDP | Confirms `e.preventDefault()` + `e.stopPropagation()` work | | **Edge Cases** (2) | Sets/bundles excluded (btn count ≤ tile count), sequential modal open/close works | Guards against state leak between modals |  ### Key Design Decisions - **Three-outcome assertion pattern** for every modal open (content / error-state / crash page) — no silent 15s timeouts - **`force: true` clicks** on Quick View buttons since desktop viewport has `opacity: 0` by default (revealed on hover) - **`domcontentloaded` + explicit locator waits** throughout — zero `networkidle` or `waitForTimeout` usage - **Graceful fallback PLP navigation** — tries `/category/newarrivals` then `/category/womens` - **Crash page detection** after every navigation action
